channel.title: Pain - Chronic on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Pain - Chronic on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Willow Bark Extract and Aspirin have many health benefits (Vitamin D might be better)
item.description:
• The Broad-Spectrum Antiviral Effects of Willow Bark Extract - Mercola
      • Which health problems does willowbark or aspirin help with (Asked Chat-GPT Dec 2023)
      • Willowbark forms: liquid, pills, tea, cream, etc

The Broad-Spectrum Antiviral Effects of Willow Bark Extract - Mercola

  • Willow bark is perhaps most widely known as aspirin’s forerunner, but it appears to have powerful antiviral effects as well

  • Willow bark extract has broad-spectrum, antiviral effects against coronaviruses and enteroviruses

  • Willow bark is effective against both enveloped and nonenveloped viruses, causing the viruses to cluster together rather than spread apart

item.title: Central Nervous System protection by Vitamin D
item.description:
• Molecular Regulation of the CNS by Vitamin D
      • VitaminDWiki - Brain and Vitamin D - many studies
      • VitaminDWiki - How Vitamin D fights Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, Multiple Sclerosis and Epilepsy – June 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - Variety of evidence that Vitamin D helps the brain – July 2014 chart

Molecular Regulation of the CNS by Vitamin D

Physiol Res 2023 Dec 17;72(S4):S339-S356.

item.title: Fibromyalgia pain, fatigue, etc. reduced by 50,000 IU of Vitamin D for 12 weeks
item.description:
• The effect of vitamin D treatment on quality of life in patients with fibromyalgia
      • VitaminDWiki - Overview Fibromyalgia or Chronic Fatigue and vitamin D

The effect of vitamin D treatment on quality of life in patients with fibromyalgia

Irish Journal of Medical Science (1971 -) https://doi.org/10.1007/s11845-023-03521-4

Sedef Ersoy, Fatma Nur Kesiktas, Busra Sirin, Derya Bugdayci & Nurdan Paker (Turkey)

item.title: Pain relieved by Vitamin D if trial lasted more than 5 months (endometriosis, 50K weekly for 12 months)
item.description:
Another endometriosis-related pain trial lasting only 3 months did not result in a benefit.
It takes at least 5 months for vitamin D dosing to fully respond without a loading dose
Loading doses, on the other hand, result in a response/benefit in < 1 month

      • Antioxidant vitamins supplementation reduce endometriosis related pelvic pain in humans: a systematic review and meta-analysis
      • See also in VitaminDWiki - Endometriosis treated, and perhaps prevented, by vitamin D - many studies

Antioxidant vitamins supplementation reduce endometriosis related pelvic pain in humans: a systematic review and meta-analysis

Reproductive Biology and Endocrinology Vol 21, # 79 (2023) https://doi.org/10.1186/s12958-023-01126-1

item.title: Less morphine recall if preceded by twice a week Vitamin D (400 IU per kg in rats, human weights 80 kg)
item.description:
• Attenuation of morphine conditioned place preference and reinstatement by vitamin D
      • 40,500 hits for (morphine OR opioids OR opiates OR addiction) “vitamin D” in Google Scholar Dec 2023
      • Wikipedia description of Conditioned place preference

Attenuation of morphine conditioned place preference and reinstatement by vitamin D

Behav Pharmacol. 2023 Aug 15. doi: 10.1097/FBP.0000000000000747

Mahdieh Akbari 1, Houman Parsaei 2, Katayoun Sedaghat 1, Fatemeh Mousavi 1

  • 1 Research Center of Physiology, Semnan University of Medical Sciences, Semnan and.

item.title: Pain not reduced by 60,000 IU monthly vitamin D (need 50,000 IU weekly) – RCT
item.description:
• The effect of vitamin D supplementation on pain: an analysis of data from the D-Health randomised controlled trial

The effect of vitamin D supplementation on pain: an analysis of data from the D-Health randomised controlled trial

Trial Br J Nutr. 2023 Aug 28;130(4):633-640. doi: 10.1017/S000711452200356

Aninda Rahman 1, Mary Waterhouse 2, Catherine Baxter 2, Briony Duarte Romero 2, Donald S A McLeod 2 3, Bruce K Armstrong 4, Peter R Ebeling 5, Dallas R English 6 7, Gunter Hartel 2, Michael G Kimlin 8, Rachel O’Connell 9, Jolieke C van der Pols 10, Alison J Venn 11, Penelope M Webb 2 12, David C Whiteman 2 12, Rachel E Neale 2 12

item.title: Muscle pain in obese women reduced by aerobic exercise plus 50,000 IU Vitamin D - RCT
item.description:
• Efficacy of Vitamin D Supplementation in Addition to Aerobic Exercise Training in Obese Women with Perceived Myalgia: A Single-Blinded Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial

Efficacy of Vitamin D Supplementation in Addition to Aerobic Exercise Training in Obese Women with Perceived Myalgia: A Single-Blinded Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial

Nutrients 2021, 13(6), 1819; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu13061819 Egypt

**If 2X- 3X larger doses of Vitamin D,

40 ng and more decrease in pain
    100,000 to 150,000 IU per week**

item.title: Large dose Vitamin D before surgery was found to help by 35 studies
item.description:
This small study would probably have gotten statistically significant results if they have given the 300,000 IU of Vitamin D 3 days BEFORE the surgery. It takes several days for oral vitamin D to get to the cells (injection is slower, nanoemulsion is faster)

      • Optimal vitamin D status at admission appears to improve survival after craniotomy in patients with brain malignancies

Optimal vitamin D status at admission appears to improve survival after craniotomy in patients with brain malignancies

Clinical Nutrition ESPEN Vol 55, June 2023, Pages 428-433 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.clnesp.2023.04.009 $25 paywall

item.title: Knee osteoarthritis treated by Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
• Knee Osteoarthritis 1.7 more likely if low Vitamin D - Jan 2024
      • Both Vitamin D and Magnesium appear to be needed if have Knee Osteoarthritis - Oct 2023
      • Knee osteoarthritis pain reduced by 4,000 IU of Vitamin D for 3 months (35 ng avg.) – Dec 2022** The influence of vitamin D supplementation on the expression of mediators of inflammation in knee osteoarthritis **Immunol Res . 2022 Dec 26. doi: 10.1007/s12026-022-09354-0
      • Vitamin D reduces pain and cartilage destruction in knee osteoarthritis animals through inhibiting the matrix metalloprotease (MMPs) expression - May 2023

Knee Osteoarthritis 1.7 more likely if low Vitamin D - Jan 2024

Relationship between serum vitamin D levels and the prevalence of knee osteoarthritis: A retrospective study on 3424 subjects

item.title: More post-operative pain if low vitamin D before ( 2.4 X for Thoracoscopic)
item.description:
• Preoperative Vitamin D Level is Associated with Acute Pain After Video-Assisted Thoracoscopic Surgery: A Retrospective Cohort Study
      • Search Google Scholar for pain postoperative “vitamin d” (34.100 as of Oct 2022)

Preoperative Vitamin D Level is Associated with Acute Pain After Video-Assisted Thoracoscopic Surgery: A Retrospective Cohort Study

J Pain Res. 2022 Oct 12;15:3189-3196.doi: 10.2147/JPR.S382407

Xiaoping Zeng # 1 , Xue Chen # 1 , Caixia Li 2 , Hong Shi 1

Purpose: Low vitamin D levels have been associated with musculoskeletal pain, cancer pain, chronic postoperative pain, and post-traumatic pain. However, their association with postoperative pain after video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery has not been explored. The aim of this study was to examine the association between vitamin D levels and postoperative pain after video-assisted thoracoscopic surgery.

item.title: Fibromyalgia Syndrome correlated with poor Vitamin D Receptor (less D to cells) in a small study
item.description:
• Relationship Between Vitamin D Receptor Gene BsmI Polymorphism and Fibromyalgia Syndrome
      • See also in VitaminDWiki - Overview Fibromyalgia or Chronic Fatigue and vitamin D

Relationship Between Vitamin D Receptor Gene BsmI Polymorphism and Fibromyalgia Syndrome

Cureus . 2022 Jul 21;14(7) doi: 10.7759/cureus.27113

Sidrah Parvez 1 , Ghizal Fatima 1 , Farzana Mehdi 2 , Najah R Hadi 3 , Jan Fedacko 4

item.title: Fibromyalgia and Musculoskeletal Pain treated by raised Vitamin D levels
item.description:
• The Efficacy of Vitamin D Supplementation in the Treatment of Fibromyalgia Syndrome and Chronic Musculoskeletal Pain
      • VitaminDWiki - Overview Fibromyalgia or Chronic Fatigue and vitamin D

The Efficacy of Vitamin D Supplementation in the Treatment of Fibromyalgia Syndrome and Chronic Musculoskeletal Pain

Nutrients 2022, 14(15), 3010; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu14153010

by Mauro Lombardo 1,*ORCID,Alessandra Feraco 1,2,Morena Ottaviani 1,Gianluca Rizzo 3ORCID,Elisabetta Camajani 1,4ORCID,Massimiliano Caprio 1,2ORCID andAndrea Armani 1,2

item.title: Ageing faster and more knee pain are associated with low vitamin D
item.description:
• Accelerated Epigenetic Aging Mediates the Association between Vitamin D Levels and Knee Pain in Community-Dwelling Individuals
      • Some related Aging studies on the web

Accelerated Epigenetic Aging Mediates the Association between Vitamin D Levels and Knee Pain in Community-Dwelling Individuals

The journal of nutrition, health & aging (2022) https://doi.org/10.1007/s12603-022-1758-z

L. J. Strath, L. Meng, A. Rani, P. Sinha, A. J. Johnson, Z. Huo, T. C. Foster, J. D. Edburg, R. B. Fillingim & Yenisel Cruz-Almeida

item.title: Epstein Barr Virus and Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
item.description:
• Early Growth Response Gene Upregulation in Epstein–Barr Virus (EBV)-Associated Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (ME/CFS)

Early Growth Response Gene Upregulation in Epstein–Barr Virus (EBV)-Associated Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (ME/CFS)

Biomolecules 2020, 10(11), 1484; https://doi.org/10.3390/biom10111484

by Jonathan Kerr. Dept of Microbiology, Norfolk & Norwich University Hospital (NNUH), Colney Lane

Myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS) is a chronic multisystem disease exhibiting a variety of symptoms and affecting multiple systems. Psychological stress and virus infection are important. Virus infection may trigger the onset, and psychological stress may reactivate latent viruses, for example, Epstein–Barr virus (EBV). It has recently been reported that EBV induced gene 2 (EBI2) was upregulated in blood in a subset of ME/CFS patients. The purpose of this study was to determine whether the pattern of expression of early growth response (EGR) genes, important in EBV infection and which have also been found to be upregulated in blood of ME/CFS patients, paralleled that of EBI2. EGR gene upregulation was found to be closely associated with that of EBI2 in ME/CFS, providing further evidence in support of ongoing EBV reactivation in a subset of ME/CFS patients. EGR1, EGR2, and EGR3 are part of the cellular immediate early gene response and are important in EBV transcription, reactivation, and B lymphocyte transformation. EGR1 is a regulator of immune function, and is important in vascular homeostasis, psychological stress, connective tissue disease, mitochondrial function, all of which are relevant to ME/CFS. EGR2 and EGR3 are negative regulators of T lymphocytes and are important in systemic autoimmunity.

item.title: Nightmares associated with low Vitamin D and low Calcium
Less labor pain if higher level of vitamin D
Pain reduced by Vitamin D (60K weekly, then monthly) plus daily Calcium in 3 months
item.description:
Chronic, Non-Specific Pain Helped with Vitamin D- Grassroots Health on this study

VitaminDWiki - Weekly, Monthly Vitamin D are typically better than daily - many studies

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Background and Objective: Despite abundant sunshine, India is a country with high prevalence of vitamin D deficiency. It has been suggested that vitamin D deficiency could be a potential cause of chronic non-specific musculoskeletal pain. The study was conducted to evaluate the effect of supplementation of vitamin D and calcium on patients suffering with chronic non-specific musculoskeletal pain.

item.title: Opioid addiction related to low vitamin D in many ways (in both mice and men)
item.description:
• Vitamin D deficiency exacerbates UV/endorphin and opioid addiction
      • Opioid crisis cost US $1.5 Trillion in 2020 (increase of $0.5 trillion since 2019)

Vitamin D deficiency exacerbates UV/endorphin and opioid addiction

Sci Adv. 2021 Jun 11;7(24):eabe4577. doi: 10.1126/sciadv.abe4577

Lajos V Kemény 1, Kathleen C Robinson 1, Andrea L Hermann 1, Deena M Walker 2, Susan Regan 3, Yik Weng Yew 4, Yi Chun Lai 5, Nicholas Theodosakis 1, Phillip D Rivera 6 7 8, Weihua Ding 9, Liuyue Yang 9, Tobias Beyer 1, Yong-Hwee E Loh 2 10, Jennifer A Lo 1, Anita A J van der Sande 1, William Sarnie 1, David Kotler 1, Jennifer J Hsiao 1, Mack Y Su 1, Shinichiro Kato 1, Joseph Kotler 1, Staci D Bilbo 6 7, Vanita Chopra 11, Matthew P Salomon 12, Shiqian Shen 9, Dave S B Hoon 12, Maryam M Asgari 13, Sarah E Wakeman 3, Eric J Nestler 2, David E Fisher 14

item.title: 15,000 US death per year from NSAIDs (0 deaths from Vitamin D)
item.description:
• Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs
    • Vitamin D is steroid which has anti-inflammatory properties
      • Aspirin: 3,000 US deaths per years (mainly seniors) - 2017
      • The dangers of NSAIDs: look both ways - April 2016
      • Tylenol/Acetaminophen is the cause of 500 deaths annually in the US - kidney damage

Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs

(NSAIDs) are medicines that are widely used to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and bring down a high temperature. They’re often used to relieve symptoms of headaches, painful periods, sprains and strains, colds and flu, arthritis, and other causes of long-term pain.

item.title: Vitamin D interplay with pain
Opiate addiction 2.4 X more likely if vitamin D deficient before surgery
item.description:
Perioperative Serum 25-Hydroxyvitamin D Levels as a Predictor of Postoperative Opioid Use and Opioid Use Disorder: A Cohort Study

J Gen Intern Med. 2020 Jun 24. doi: 10.1007/s11606-020-06001-y.

Yuhree Kim 1 2, Fang Zhang 1, Katherine Su 1 2, Marc LaRochelle 3, Matthew Callahan 1, David Fisher 2, J Frank Wharam 1, Maryam M Asgari 4 5

  • Reduced palliative cancer pain after Vitamin D supplementation – April 2016

item.title: Chronic pain if osteoporosis and low vitamin D
3X less Septic Shock in children with sepsis getting 150,000 IU of Vitamin D - RCT
item.description:
Effects of a single dose of vitamin D in septic children: a randomized, double-blinded, controlled trial

Journal of International Medical Research https://doi.org/10.1177/0300060520926890

Yu Wang, Zhongwen Yang, Li Gao, Zhenfeng Cao, Qianhan Wang

Septic Shock: 6.7% vs 20%
Vitamin D levels 12 ng ==> 22 ng
If enough vitamin D had been given to raise the levels to >30 ng (300,000 IU?)
    perhaps 5X less Septic Shock instead of 3X

item.title: Opioid addiction reduced by Omega-3 (gut inflammation in mice)
item.description:
• Dietary Supplementation with Omega-3 Polyunsaturated Fatty Acids Reduces Opioid-Seeking Behaviors and Alters the Gut Microbiome.
      • See also VitaminDWiki

Dietary Supplementation with Omega-3 Polyunsaturated Fatty Acids Reduces Opioid-Seeking Behaviors and Alters the Gut Microbiome.

Hakimian JK1, Dong TS2, Barahona JA1, Lagishetty V2, Tiwari S1, Azani D1, Barrera M1, Lee S1, Severino AL1, Mittal N3, Cahill CM1, Jacobs JP2, Walwyn WM1

Nutrients, 13 Aug 2019, 11(8) DOI: 10.3390/nu11081900

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

item.title: Dysmenorrhea substantially reduced by Ginger, just 1,000 IU Vitamin D also helped – RCT
item.description:
Comparison of the effect of vitamin E, vitamin D and ginger on the severity of primary dysmenorrhea: a single-blind clinical trial

Obstet Gynecol Sci. 2019 Nov;62(6):462-468. https://doi.org/10.5468/ogs.2019.62.6.462

Vitamin D would probably have been much BETTER than Ginger if the study had used more vitamin D (say 4,000 IU daily) and/or a loading dose (400,000 IU)

  • Menstrual Pain (PMS) reduced by vitamin D – RCT 2012, 2014, 2016

item.title: Fatigued seniors have lower levels of Vitamin D
item.description:
Decrease in Serum Vitamin D Level of Older Patients with Fatigue

Nutrients 2019, 11(10), 2531; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu11102531

Manuela Pennisi 1,Giulia Malaguarnera 1,2,Giuseppe Di Bartolo 2,Giuseppe Lanza 3,4,Rita Bella 5,Eleonora Margherita Chisari 6,Omar Cauli 7,Enzo Vicari 8 andMichele Malaguarnera 2,7,

(This article belongs to the Special Issue Diet and Fatigue)

  • Overview Fibromyalgia or Chronic Fatigue and vitamin D

item.title: Vitality recaptured with Vitamin D (stories of Swedish immigrant women in this case)
item.description:
• Quality of Life

    • Vitamin D supplementation improved quality of life – Aug 2015

  • 3 to 55 X more likely to have these health problems if low Vitamin D

  • Incidence of 22 health problems related to vitamin D have doubled in a decade

item.title: Long-term neck and back pains greatly reduced by 50,000 IU of Vitamin D – 4 cases
Neuropathy treated by vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
• Diabetic Neuropathy treated by 5,000 IU of Vitamin D daily (more than 8 weeks would be far better) - RCT Dec 2021
      • Complementary and Alternative Medicine for Painful Peripheral Neuropathy - 2019
      • Google Scholar “Peripheral Neuropathy” “Vitamin D” 18,600 items March 2023
      • Google Scholar “Peripheral Neuropathy” and “Vitamin B” 12,700 items March 2023

Diabetic Neuropathy treated by 5,000 IU of Vitamin D daily (more than 8 weeks would be far better) - RCT Dec 2021

The Benefits of Add-on Therapy of Vitamin D 5000 IU to the Vitamin D Levels and Symptoms in Diabetic Neuropathy Patients: A Randomized Clinical Trial

item.title: 8 Signs of Low Vitamin D - sweating, bones ache, muscle pain, poor immune, fatigue, slow healing etc. – YouTube
item.description:
8 Signs Your Body Is Begging for Vitamin D Bright Side 8 minute cartoon video

What are the signs of vitamin D deficiency? Vitamin D greatly influences the systems of our body from ensuring calcium supply to our bones to strengthening our immune system. We normally get this vitamin from foods, supplements, and the sun’s rays, but when something goes wrong and we face vitamin D deficiency, our body systems may break.

item.title: Fibromyalgia severity associated with serotonin but not Vitamin D (almost all were insufficient)
item.description:
Clinical association of vitamin D and serotonin levels among patients with fibromyalgia syndrome

Neuropsychiatric Disease and Treatment Volume 2019:15 Pages 1421—1426, DOI https://doi.org/10.2147/NDT.S198434

  • Overview Fibromyalgia or Chronic Fatigue and vitamin D

“Large doses of vitamin D reduced CFS/Fibromyalgia in > 10 studies”

“Many books and online forums recommend vitamin D for CFS/Fibromyalgia”

  • Fibromyalgia associated with lower Vitamin D – meta-analysis Oct 2017

item.title: Off topic: Transcutaneous enhancers (DMSO, etc) for Vitamin D, Vitamin C, Resveratrol, Magnesium, etc.
item.description:
DMSO is able to enhance “small sized” molecules (< 500 daltons) getting through the skin

Have not yet found out the DMSO “speedup factor”

DMSO was made in 1860 and its transdermal properties were discovered about 40 years later

High concentration DMSO has used on the skin for various purposed 4 times a day for a month

DMSO by itself has various health benefits

The 500 Dalton Rule of Dermal Penetration and Cosmetic Science March 2017

item.title: Low Level Laser Therapy greatly increased Vitamin D and Magnesium (for diabetics with nephropathy)
item.description:
There are many conferences each year on the topic

October 24-28, 2022: Photodynamic Therapy and Photodiagnosis Update 2022, Nancy, France

June 23-28, 2022: 18th International Photodynamic Association World Congress, Moscow, Russia

August 30 - September 3, 2021: 19th Congress of the European Society for Photobiology, Salzburg, Austria

November 5 - 6, 2020: Photodynamic Therapy and Photodiagnosis Update, E-CONGRESS

July 28 - 30, 2020: The American Society for Photobiology (ASP) 2020 Virtual Meeting, Chicago, USA

item.title: Bath with Magnesium Chloride probably better than Epsom Salts
item.description:
Found lots of opinions and statements but unable to quickly find any good data - Jan 2019

Myth or Reality-Transdermal Magnesium? – July 2017

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

In Feb 2019 I purchased Oraganix Magnesium Salt Bath Flakes 8 lb, $15 Amazon

  • “Magnesium Chloride is better absorbed than its counterpart Epsom Salt (Magnesium Sulfate)”

  • “Dissolve 1-3 cups of the magnesium flakes into a warm footbath or full tub bath, and soak for 30 minutes or longer.

item.title: Vitamin B12 as a Treatment for Pain – review
item.description:
• Dr. Coimbra discusses Vitamin D, Magnesium, Folic Acid, B12, Autism, Depression, etc – Sept 2018

  • Autism treated by Vitamin B12 - several studies

  • Sleep, Vitamin D, Vitamin B-12, IBS, Fibromyalgia - Gominak March 2015

    • Hypothesis – Vitamin B-12 deficiency is associated with Vitamin D deficiency Gominak

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

item.title: Rheumatoid Arthritis pain reduced by monthly 100,000 IU of Vitamin D
item.description:
An exploratory study on the role of vitamin D supplementation in improving pain and disease activity in rheumatoid arthritis.

Mod Rheumatol. 2018 Oct 4:1-8. doi: 10.1080/14397595.2018.1532622.

Adami G1, Rossini M1, Bogliolo L2, Cantatore FP3, Varenna M4, Malavolta N5, Del Puente A6, Muratore M7, Orsolini G1, Gatti D1, Viapiana O1; Study Group on Osteoporosis and Metabolic Skeletal Diseases of the Italian Society of Rheumatology (SIR).

PDF is available free at Sci-Hub   10.1080/14397595.2018.1532622
